overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved site plans for the new Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and adopted a new salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. The commission also scheduled the first steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan update for June 1st.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for Wellhead Protection District
April 2026 staff approved certified survey map and site plan
Planning and development department monthly project update report

discussions.item: Site plan review - Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive. The building was rotated and repositioned north to accommodate a 36-inch gas pipeline running under the parking lot and to reduce fill costs. Chief Jeremy explained the building will accommodate 50-60 officers for future growth beyond current 40 officers and 4 clerical staff. Discussion addressed an alternate storage shed placement, with commissioners suggesting moving it further north to allow for future building expansion. The landscaping plan meets current relaxed code requirements. A motion to recommend approval to the Board of Trustees with staff conditions 2-5 passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for Wellhead Protection District
discussions.body: Staff proposed a new inspection program with two annual inspections per facility - a warm weather inspection focused on structural integrity and drainage, and a winter operational inspection focused on material handling and containment. A standardized inspection worksheet similar to DOT standards was developed. Staff noted one existing facility near the municipal well on Sternberg has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and will be contacted about compliance. The motion to approve the program with reporting back to both Plan Commission and Public Works Committee passed unanimously.
Motion to approve as presented was made and seconded. Approved unanimously without discussion.

discussions.item: April 2026 staff approved certified survey map and site plan
Planning and development department monthly update
discussions.body: Staff announced the first joint Plan Commission and Village Board steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 PM. Staff will send email notifications and calendar invites.

discussions.item: Future agenda items and commissioner remarks
discussions.body: Electric vehicle charging stations will be added as a future agenda item, likely for fall. A commissioner inquired about potential new TIF districts for residential/workforce housing. Staff mentioned Ehlers has prepared a presentation on residential TIF qualifications that could be shared with the commission, possibly as part of the comprehensive plan process.

publicComment: Jim Pensel of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via online participation. He urged the commission to exercise common sense regarding landscaping requirements for the police department project to avoid excessive taxpayer costs, referencing relaxed standards used for the Greenheck Turner building. He also requested that the salt shed inspection program include annual reporting on site visits and findings.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive recommended for approval to Board of Trustees with staff conditions
Salt storage shed inspection program adopted for wellhead protection district with twice-annual inspections
Staff to report inspection program results to Plan Commission and Public Works Committee next year
Staff to contact existing salt storage facility near Sternberg about wellhead protection compliance
First comprehensive plan steering committee meeting scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 PM
Staff to send email and calendar invites for steering committee meeting
Electric vehicle charging stations to be added to future agenda for fall discussion
Staff to share Ehlers presentation on residential TIF district qualifications with commission
title: Plan Commission
